description: To meet a need or desire adequately; to bring to a state of contentment. 'Heno' names the meeting of need—enough arriving, want dissolving.
sound_symbolism: The word settles gently. 'He' breathes with soft ease, tension releasing. 'No' settles with grounded enough, contentment found.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb: 'mi heno' (I satisfy). Takes object being satisfied. Related to 'korei' (fulfill) but emphasizes contentment over completion.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Satisfaction as enough—recognizing when needs are met, releasing endless wanting.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Satisfying communication—exchanges that meet the needs of all participants.
